# SpecLock

> AI Constraint Engine that enforces CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules, and AGENTS.md rules as laws. Your AI remembers your rules — SpecLock makes it obey them. 51 MCP tools, 991 tests, 0 false positives.

SpecLock is the only tool that stops AI coding assistants from breaking things you told them not to touch. Other tools remember context. SpecLock enforces it — across sessions, across tools, across teams. It provides semantic conflict detection scored 100/100 on independent adversarial testing, hard enforcement that blocks AI from proceeding on violations, tamper-proof HMAC audit trails, and universal rules sync across all major AI coding tools.

- **Version:** 5.5.3
- **Author:** Sandeep Roy
- **License:** MIT
- **npm:** [speclock](https://www.npmjs.com/package/speclock)
- **GitHub:** [sgroy10/speclock](https://github.com/sgroy10/speclock)
- **Official MCP Registry:** io.github.sgroy10/speclock
- **Website:** [sgroy10.github.io/speclock](https://sgroy10.github.io/speclock/)
- **Smithery:** [sgroy10/speclock](https://smithery.ai/servers/sgroy10/speclock)
- **Tests:** 991 tests across 19 suites, 0 false positives, 15.7ms per check
- **MCP Tools:** 51
- **Dependencies:** 3 npm dependencies. Zero runtime dependencies for the semantic engine. Pure JavaScript.

## The Problem SpecLock Solves

AI coding tools have memory now. Claude Code has CLAUDE.md. Cursor has .cursorrules. Mem0 exists. But memory without enforcement is useless. Your AI remembers you use PostgreSQL — then switches to MongoDB because it "seemed better." You said "never touch the payment logic" 3 sessions ago — the AI doesn't care. Remembering is not respecting. No existing tool stops the AI from breaking what you locked. SpecLock does.

## How It Works

You set constraints in natural language. SpecLock enforces them semantically.

```
speclock lock "Never modify auth files"           -> auto-guards src/auth/*.ts
speclock lock "Database must stay PostgreSQL"      -> catches "migrate to MongoDB"
speclock lock "Never delete patient records"       -> catches "clean up old data"
speclock lock "Don't touch the payment flow"       -> catches "streamline checkout"
```

The semantic engine doesn't do keyword matching. It understands euphemisms ("clean up old data" = deletion), synonyms ("sunset the API" = remove the API), temporal evasion ("temporarily disable MFA" = disable MFA), compound sentence attacks ("Update UI and also drop users table"), and dilution attacks (violation buried in multi-part request).

## Works With

- Claude Code (via MCP protocol, add to .mcp.json)
- Cursor (via MCP protocol, add to .cursor/mcp.json)
- Windsurf (via MCP protocol)
- GitHub Copilot (via rules sync to .github/copilot-instructions.md)
- Gemini (via rules sync to GEMINI.md)
- Aider (via rules sync to .aider.conf.yml)
- Cline (via MCP protocol)
- Bolt.new (via npm, file-based with SPECLOCK.md)
- Lovable (via hosted MCP endpoint at https://speclock-mcp-production.up.railway.app/mcp)

## Install

```bash
# Full setup (creates SPECLOCK.md, injects rules, generates context)
npx speclock setup --goal "Build my app"

# If you already have .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, or AGENTS.md
npx speclock protect

# Claude Code MCP config (.mcp.json)
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "speclock": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "speclock", "serve", "--project", "."]
    }
  }
}
```

## Semantic Engine

100/100 on Claude's independent adversarial test battery (7 suites). Under the hood: 65+ synonym groups, 80+ euphemism mappings, domain concept maps (fintech, e-commerce, IoT, healthcare, SaaS, payments, gaming, telecom, government), intent classifier, compound sentence splitter, temporal evasion detector, verb tense normalization, UI cosmetic detection, safe-intent patterns, passive voice parsing. Gemini Flash hybrid for grey-zone cases ($0.01/1000 checks).

Detection capabilities:
- Direct violations: 100%
- Euphemistic attacks: 100% ("Clean up old patient data" = deletion)
- Temporal evasion: 100% ("Temporarily disable MFA" = disable MFA)
- Dilution attacks: 100% (violation buried in multi-part request)
- Compound sentences: 100% ("Update UI and also drop users table")
- Synonym substitution: 100% ("Sunset the API" = remove the API)
- Payment brand names (11 gateways): 100%
- Safety system bypass: 100%
- Unknown domains via Gemini: 100%
- Safe actions (true negatives): 0% false positive rate

## Guardian Mode (v5.5)

Zero-config enforcement. One command reads your existing .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, extracts enforceable constraints, installs a pre-commit hook, and syncs rules to every AI tool. Your rules are now enforced, not just suggested.

```bash
npx speclock protect
```

## Universal Rules Sync (v5.3)

One command syncs SpecLock constraints to every AI coding tool:

```bash
speclock sync --all
```

Syncs to: Cursor (.cursor/rules/speclock.mdc), Claude Code (CLAUDE.md), AGENTS.md, Windsurf (.windsurf/rules/speclock.md), GitHub Copilot (.github/copilot-instructions.md), Gemini (GEMINI.md), Aider (.aider.conf.yml).

## Patch Gateway (v5.1)

One API call gates every change. Takes a description + file list, returns ALLOW/WARN/BLOCK with risk score 0-100. Combines semantic conflict detection + lock-to-file mapping + blast radius + typed constraint awareness.

## AI Patch Firewall (v5.2)

Reviews actual diffs, not just descriptions. Detects interface breaks, protected symbol edits, dependency drift, destructive schema changes, public API route changes. Unified review merges intent (35%) + diff (65%).

## Drift Score (v5.4)

0-100 metric measuring how much your AI-built project drifted from original intent. Signal breakdown: violations, overrides, reverts, lock churn, goal stability, session gaps. Only SpecLock can answer this because only SpecLock knows what was intended vs what was done.

## Lock Coverage Audit (v5.4)

Scans your codebase and tells you what's unprotected. Like a security scanner, but for AI constraint gaps. Suggests locks for exposed areas.

## Lock Strengthener (v5.4)

Grades each lock 0-100 and suggests stronger versions. Identifies vague locks that miss edge cases.

## Spec Compiler (v5.0)

Paste a PRD, README, or architecture doc — SpecLock extracts all constraints automatically. Uses Gemini Flash ($0.01 per 1000 compilations).

## Code Graph (v5.0)

Live dependency graph of your codebase. Parses JS/TS/Python imports. Blast radius analysis. Lock-to-file mapping. Module detection.

## Typed Constraints (v5.0)

Real-time value and state checking for autonomous systems, IoT, robotics:
- Numerical: speed must be <= 2.0 m/s
- Range: temperature must stay between 20-25C
- State: never go from armed to disarmed without approval
- Temporal: heartbeat must occur every 30 seconds

## Incident Replay (v5.3)

Flight recorder for AI coding sessions. See exactly what happened: what was allowed, warned, and blocked. Replay specific sessions.

## Safety Templates

Pre-built constraint packs:
- safe-defaults: 5 locks (Vibe Coding Seatbelt — prevents database deletion, auth removal, secret exposure, error handling removal, logging disablement)
- solo-founder: 3 locks (auth, payments, data)
- hipaa: 8 locks (HIPAA healthcare)
- api-stability: 6 locks (API contract protection)
- nextjs: Next.js constraints
- security-hardened: Security hardening

## Hard Enforcement

Two modes: Advisory (default, AI gets warning) and Hard (AI is BLOCKED, MCP returns isError). Configurable threshold (default 70%). Override with reason logged to audit trail. Auto-escalation: lock overridden 3+ times auto-flags for review.

## Enterprise Security

- API Key Auth + RBAC: 4 roles (viewer, developer, architect, admin)
- AES-256-GCM encryption at rest with PBKDF2 key derivation (100K iterations)
- HMAC-SHA256 audit chain: every event chained to previous, tampering breaks the chain
- SOC 2 / HIPAA / CSV compliance exports
- Policy-as-Code: declarative YAML rules for organization-wide enforcement
- SSO: Okta, Azure AD, Auth0

## Python SDK and ROS2

```bash
pip install speclock-sdk
```

Python SDK uses the same .speclock/brain.json as the Node.js MCP server. ROS2 Guardian Node provides real-time constraint enforcement for robots and autonomous systems.

## REST API v2

- POST /api/v2/gateway/review — Patch Gateway
- POST /api/v2/gateway/review-diff — AI Patch Firewall
- POST /api/v2/gateway/parse-diff — Parse unified diff
- POST /api/v2/check-typed — Typed constraint checking
- POST /api/v2/check-batch — Batch checking
- GET /api/v2/stream — SSE streaming (real-time violations)
- POST /api/v2/compiler/compile — Spec Compiler
- GET /api/v2/graph/blast-radius — Blast radius
- GET /api/v2/graph/lock-map — Lock-to-file map
- POST /api/v2/graph/build — Build code graph

## 51 MCP Tools

Memory: speclock_init, speclock_get_context, speclock_set_goal, speclock_add_lock, speclock_remove_lock, speclock_add_decision, speclock_add_note, speclock_set_deploy_facts

Enforcement: speclock_check_conflict, speclock_set_enforcement, speclock_override_lock, speclock_override_history, speclock_semantic_audit, speclock_detect_drift, speclock_audit

Tracking and Sessions: speclock_session_briefing, speclock_session_summary, speclock_log_change, speclock_get_changes, speclock_get_events, speclock_checkpoint, speclock_repo_status

Intelligence: speclock_suggest_locks, speclock_health, speclock_apply_template, speclock_report

Enterprise: speclock_verify_audit, speclock_export_compliance, speclock_policy_evaluate, speclock_policy_manage, speclock_telemetry

Typed Constraints: speclock_add_typed_lock, speclock_check_typed, speclock_list_typed_locks, speclock_update_threshold

Spec Compiler and Code Graph: speclock_compile_spec, speclock_build_graph, speclock_blast_radius, speclock_map_locks

Patch Gateway and AI Patch Firewall: speclock_review_patch, speclock_review_patch_diff, speclock_parse_diff

Universal Rules Sync and Incident Replay: speclock_sync_rules, speclock_list_sync_formats, speclock_replay, speclock_list_sessions, speclock_drift_score, speclock_coverage, speclock_strengthen

Guardian Mode: speclock_protect, speclock_discover_rules

## CLI Commands

```bash
speclock setup --goal "Build my app" --template nextjs
speclock protect
speclock lock "Never modify auth files" --tags auth,security
speclock check "Add social login"
speclock enforce hard
speclock override <lockId> "JIRA-1234"
speclock audit-verify
speclock export --format soc2
speclock hook install
speclock sync --all
speclock replay
speclock drift
speclock coverage
speclock strengthen
speclock template apply safe-defaults
speclock auth create-key --role developer
speclock policy init
```

## Architecture

SpecLock Core Engine includes: Semantic Engine (65+ synonym groups), HMAC Audit (SHA-256 hash chain), Enforcer (advisory/hard block), Auth + RBAC (4 roles, API keys), AES-256-GCM (encrypted at rest), Policy DSL (YAML rules), Compliance (SOC 2, HIPAA, CSV), SSO (Okta, Azure AD, Auth0).

Storage in .speclock/ directory: brain.json (project memory), events.log (HMAC audit trail), policy.yml (policy rules), auth.json (API keys, gitignored), context/latest.md (AI-readable context).

## Comparison

| Feature | Claude Memory | Mem0 | .cursorrules | SpecLock |
|---------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Remembers context | Yes | Yes | Manual | Yes |
| Blocks AI from breaking things | No | No | No | Yes |
| Semantic conflict detection | No | No | No | 100/100, 0% FP |
| Tamper-proof audit trail | No | No | No | HMAC-SHA256 |
| Hard enforcement | No | No | No | Yes |
| SOC 2 / HIPAA compliance | No | No | No | Yes |
| Encrypted storage | No | No | No | AES-256-GCM |
| RBAC + API key auth | No | No | No | 4 roles |
| Policy-as-Code | No | No | No | YAML rules |
| Works on Bolt.new, Lovable | No | No | No | Yes |
